lines.text: Originally written by Michael Borkow, Mike Sikowitz & Jeffrey Astrof
In a TV commercial that the gang is watching at Monica and Rachel's.
[A guy is sitting at his desk and his boss comes in and drops a huge pile of papers on his desk. The guy looks dejected.]

lines.speaker: Commercial Voiceover
lines.text: Can't get the monkey off your back? Then put it in your mouth...
[A monkey jumps on the desk and hands the guy a beer. He opens the beer and is suddenly on the beach, in a hammock, with beautiful women all around.]

lines.speaker: Commercial Voiceover
lines.text: ...With MonkeyShine Beer. [MonkeyShine theme] MonkeyShine Beer, 'cause it's a jungle out there.]
[Camera pans back from the TV to show the gang watching.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That commercial always makes me so sad.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but then the guy opens his beer and those girls run at him, so, everything seems to work out OK.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I meant because the monkey in it reminds me of Marcel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can see that, 'cause they both have those big brown eyes and, ya know, the little pouty chin.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And the fact that they're both monkeys.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sometimes I wonder if I did the right thing, ya know, giving him away.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Ross, you had to, I mean, he was humping everything in sight. I mean, I have a Malibu Barbi that will no longer be wearing white to her wedding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Remember when sometimes he'd borrow your hat, and, and when you got it back there'd be little monkey raisins in it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well sure, when he did it, it was funny. When I did it to my boss's hat. . . all of the sudden I have this big attitude problem.
OPENING TITLES
Central Perk. Chandler, Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe are at the couch.
[Joey enters holding a letter]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, check it out, guess what I got.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Rhythm?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, my first fan mail.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Alright!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: [reading] 'Dear Dr. Remore, know that I love you and would do anything to have you.' Gosh. 'Your not-so-secretive admirer, Erica Ford.' Ooh wait, 'PS enclosed please find 14 of my eyelashes.'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ya know, in crazy world, that means you're married.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This wasn't addressed to Days of Our Lives, this is, this came to your apartment. There's no stamp on it, this woman was in our building.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my god, I got my very own stalker.
[Ross enters with a suitcase]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, where are you off to, Travelin' Jake?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, there's this, uh, paleontology conference in L.A. so I figured I'd go and then drive down to the zoo and surprise Marcel.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know I think he will be surprised, 'till he realizes he's a monkey, and uh, you know, isn't capable of that emotion.
[Rob (Chris Isaac) enters]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Phoebe, that really cute guy is here again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, oh, OK, so everyone, pretend like I'm telling you a story, OK. And, and it's really funny. So everyone just laugh, now.
[Everyone laughs]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I know. [to Rob] Hello.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: Hi. I'm Rob Dohnen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi Rob Dohnen.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: I don't know anything about music, but I think you're really, really great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, wow.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: Anyway, I schedule performers for the childrens libraries around the city and I was just thinking, have you ever thought about playing your songs for kids?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I would love to have kids. . . you're, you're the, you're, me play the songs that I will write for them.
Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are standing around in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, whaddya wanna do for dinner?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well we could just stay in and cook for ourselves. [both laugh hysterically]
[door buzzer goes off]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: It's Erica.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, the stalker.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Never mind, it's open.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: [Joey grabs a frying pan] Yes, hitting her with a frying pan's a good idea. We might wanna have a backup plan, though, just in case she isn't a cartoon.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Let's get out of here.
[They run out and knock on Monica and Rachel's door]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The one time they're not home.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: OK, we'll just leave, and when we pass her on the stairs, she won't know it's me 'cause we've never met.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's how radio stars escape stalkers.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She's comin'.
[Both run back in their apartment. There's a knock at the door.]

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: It's me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, this is it, this is how we're gonna die. Ready?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait. [Opens the top of the dish soap he's holding]
[Joey opens the door and sees Erica (Brooke Shields). Joey gets a huge smile and Chandler squeezed the dish soap in the air.]

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Erica.
San Diego Zoo. Ross is at the Monkey cages.

lines.speaker: Lipson
lines.text: Hi, Dean Lipson, zoo administrator. I was told you had a question.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I uh, I can't seem to find the monkey I donated last year. He's a capuchan, answers to the name Marcel.

lines.speaker: Lipson
lines.text: Ahh, I'm afraid I have some bad news. Marcel has passed on.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, what happened?

lines.speaker: Lipson
lines.text: Well he got sick, and then he got sicker, and then he got a little better but then he died.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe this.

lines.speaker: Lipson
lines.text: I'm sorry Mr. Geller. But ya know, there's an old saying, 'Sometimes monkeys die.' It's not a great saying but it certainly is fitting today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, ya know, someone should have called me.

lines.speaker: Lipson
lines.text: I'm sorry. Look, I know this can't bring him back but here, it's just a gesture.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Zoo dollars?

lines.speaker: Lipson
lines.text: Yes, and come see the bird show at 4. The macaws wear hats. Well it's a lot cuter if your monkey hasn't just died.
Library. Phoebe is getting ready to sing for the kids. Chandler, Monica, and Rachel are there.
[Phoebe pulls out her guitar]

lines.speaker: Kids
lines.text: Ooohhh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe Joey's having lunch with his stalker. What i-, what is she like.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you remember Cathy Bates in Misery?

lines.speaker: Rachel And Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, she looks the exact opposite of that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And she's not crazy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no no no, she's a total wack job. Yeah, she thinks that Joey is actually Dr. Drake Remore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah, and I mean, he's going out with her? He can not persue this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, just because this woman thinks she can actually see Joey through the magical box in her living room doesn't mean she's not a person. I mean, does she not deserve happiness, does she not deserve love? What're you lookin' at me for? He's the one who wants to boff the maniac.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: You OK?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, uh-uh, I'm just, I'm nervous. So, you know what, maybe if I just, if I picture them all in their underwear.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: That's not a good idea, that's kinda the reason the last guy got fired.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm just, I'm, I'm, I'm used to playing for grown-ups. Ya know, they just, grown-ups drink their coffee and do their grown-up thing, ya know, and kids listen. This is a huge responsibility. What? Are you gonna kiss me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was thinkin' about it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK. [they kiss] OK, alrighty, let's play some tunes. Hi everybody, I'm Phoebe

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, um, I'm gonna play, um, some songs about grandparents, OK. [singing]
Now, grandma's a person who everyone likes,
she bought you a train and a bright, shiny bike.
But lately she hasn't been coming to dinner,
And last time you saw her she looked so much thinner.
Now, your mom and your dad said she moved to Peru,
but the truth is she died and some day you will too.
La-la-la la la-la-la la la-la-la la...
A nice restraunt. Joey and Erica are dining.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh, Drake, isn't it amazing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah it is. . . what?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Well, here we sit, devil may care, just a little while ago you were reattaching someone's spinal cord.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, that was a tricky one. In reality, that operation takes like, over 10 hours, but they only showed it for 2 minites.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Who's they?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No one.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh Drake, you are so talented, let me see those hands. Oh these hands, these beautiful hands, oh I could just eat them. . . but I won't.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good, otherwise my watch would fall off. [laughs hysterically]

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: No, seriously. These hands. These miracle, magical, life-giving hands. Oh, just to be near them, touch them, maybe even lick one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, just one. [she licks his hands rather emphatically] Wow, you're good at that.
[Some guy at another table starts choking]

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Oh my God! Someone, he's choking. Is anyone here a doctor?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Well, yes, yes, the best doctor in all of Salem, Dr. Drake Remore.
San Diege Zoo. Ross is still at the monkey cages. A janitor is sweeping.

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Meet me in the nocturnal house in 15 minutes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhh, hey look, I don't really enjoy being with other men that way. But, um, zoo dollars?

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: It's about your monkey. It's alive.
Restraunt. Joey and Erica are still there.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I don't understand, why didn't you help that man?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, cause, uhh, I'm a neurosurgeon and that was clearly a case of, uh, uh, foodal chokage. Alright, look, I got to tell you something.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: No, no no no, you don't have to tell me anything. You don't have to explain yourself to me. Ooh, who am I to question the great Dr. Drake Remore?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But that's what...

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I should just be happy to be near you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey I- [she cuts him off with a kiss]

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Hey what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's it, just hey. Like at the end of a dance, HEY! [she starts nibbling his hand] Hey. He-hey.
Library. Phoebe is singing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: [singing] There'll be times when you get older
when you'll want to sleep with people
just to make them like you. . .
But don't.
Cause that's another thing that you don't wanna do, everybody
That's another thing that you don't wanna do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Very informative!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Not at all inappropriate!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you for coming everybody. There're cookies in the back.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: That was great, the kids loved you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay, I rock.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: And you know why? Because you told the truth, and nobody ever tells kids the truth.You were incredible.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: How did you know there was a but?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I sense these things. It was either but or butter.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: The thing is, I think some of the parents, they were kinda hopin' that you'd play more songs about like, barnyard animals.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can do that.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: Because that would be fantastic. What? You wanna kiss me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thinkin' about it.
San Diego Zoo. Ross and the janitor are in the nocturnal house.

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Ahh, the bat. Ambassador of darkness, flitting out of his cave like a winged messenger, sightless spectre of the macabe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Buddy, my monkey?

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Oh, yeah, right. There was a break-in, few months back, inside job. Your monkey was taken.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God. But the zoo told me that my monkey was dead.

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: The zoo! Do you believe everything the zoo tells ya?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That, that's the only thing the zoo's ever told me.

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Of course they're gonna say he's dead. They don't want the bad publicity. It's all a great big cover-up. Do you have any idea how high up this thing goes?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That guy Lipson?

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Lipson knows. Do you have any idea who else knows?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I, I only know Lipson.

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Hmm, Lipson knows huh? Ahh, hello Mr. Opossum, enigma of the trees, upside-down denizen of the night, taunting gravity with...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Buddy, my monkey, my monkey.

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Word on the street - well, when I say street, I mean those little pretend streets they have here at the zoo.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Your monkey found a new career, in the entertainment field. That's all I know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is unbelievable.

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: So, what is this information worth to you, my friend?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you trying to get me to bribe you?

lines.speaker: Janitor
lines.text: Maybe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But you already told me everything.
Library. Ross show up with a MonkeyShine Beer poster.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Check it out, he actually is the MonkeyShine monkey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, so what're you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I guess I'm gonna call the beer company and try to find out where he is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what I did when I lost my Clydesdales.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, hi again.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Today we're gonna start with some songs about barnyard animals.
[singing] Oh, the cow in the meadow goes moo,
Oh, the cow in the meadow goes moo.
Then the farmer hits him on the head and grinds him up,
And that's how we get hamburgers.
Nooowww, chickens!
Chandler and Joey's apartment. Monica, Rachel, and the guys are watching Days of Our Lives.

lines.speaker: Tv Doctor
lines.text: You're the only one who can save her Drake.

lines.speaker: Joey On Tv
lines.text: Damnit, I'm a doctor, I'm not God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, there goes my whole belief system.
[knock at the door]

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: It's Erica.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God, quick turn off the TV.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No no no, wait, I wanna see what happens.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, I get Leslie out of the coma and then we make out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well how can that be, you were just kissing Sabrina?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, it's a world where Joey is a neuro-surgeon.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Erica, c'mon in.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: How did you get here so fast, I just saw you in Salem?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right, they uh, they choppered me in. What's up?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Ohh, and I see you're having a little party too. Is she here, huh, huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Sabrina. I know about you two. I saw you today kissing in the doctor's lounge.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's not what you think, that was...

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: You told me I was the only one. [throws a glass of water in his face]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright look, that's it. I don't think we should see each other anymore, alright. Look, I know I should have told you this a long time ago but I am not Drake Remore, OK. I'm not even a doctor, I'm an actor. I just pretend to be a doctor.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh my God. Do the people at the hospital know about this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Somebody wanna help me out here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know, I know. [Turns on the TV. Joey in on it.]

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: How, how can you be here and there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 'Cause it's a television show.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Drake, what're you getting at?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm not Drake.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right, he's not Drake, he's Hans Remore, Drake's evil twin.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Is this true?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, yes it is true. And I know this because, because he pretended to be Drake to, to sleep with me. [throws water in his face]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And then he told me he would run away with me, and he didn't. [throws water in his face]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And you left the toilet seat up, you bastard. [throws water in his face]

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Is all this true?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes, I'm afraid it is. You deserve much better than me Erica. You deserve to be with the real Drake, he's the one you fell in love with. Go to Salem, find him, he's the guy for you.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh Hans. [They kiss]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hans...Hans...Yo evil twin.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right. Goodbye Erica, good luck in Salem. Take care

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I'll never forget you Hans. [Joey shuts the door in her face]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: OK, alright, the people who threw the water.
Central Perk. Phoebe and Rob are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fired! Why?

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: The library board has had a lot of complaints from parents about some of the stuff in your songs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't believe it. Did you tell your board about how kids want to hear the truth?

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: Maybe if you just played some regular kiddie songs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. What do you, what do you want me to be, like some stupid, big, like, purple dinosaur?

lines.speaker: Rob
lines.text: I'm not saying you have to be Barney.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who's Barney.
Central Perk. The whole gang is there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I tracked down Marcel and get this, he's healthy, he's happy, and he's right here in New York filming Outbreak II - The Virus Takes Manhattan.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're kidding.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is amazing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I finally get a part on TV and the monkey's makin' movies.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, Rachel, I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK.
[little kid enters]

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.text: Excuse me. Is this where the singing lady is that tells the truth?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, yeah I guess that's me.

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.text: [shouting out the door] She's here.
[a rush of kids enter]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: [singing] Sometimes men love women,
sometimes men love men,
and then there are bisexuals,
though some just say they're kidding themselves.
la la-la-la la-la-la-la-la-la-la...
City street. The whole gang is walking up to the movie set.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is so exciting, I haven't seen my monkey in almost a year.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What, you never look down in the shower? Oh please. I'm not allowed to make one joke in the monkey-is-penis genre?

lines.speaker: Security Guard
lines.text: C'mon people, back up please, back up, c'mon, c'mon, c'mon, c'mon.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, excuse me, uh, where can we find the monkey?

lines.speaker: Security Guard
lines.text: I'm sorry guys, closed set.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, I'm sorry, you don't understand, I'm, I'm, I'm a friend of his. We uh, we used to live together.

lines.speaker: Security Guard
lines.text: Yeah, and I have a time share in the Pocanos with Flipper.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, there he is.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, hey buddy, Marcel. Marcel. [Marcel doesn't react so Ross starts singing] In the jungle, the mighty jungle the lion sleeps tonight. [no reaction from Marcel, Monica and Joey urge him on] In the jungle, the mighty jungle the lion sleeps tonight. [Marcel looks over and everyone joins in] a-weema-way, a-weema-way..... [Marcel runs over and hops up on Ross's shoulder]
The next time at the movie set.

lines.speaker: Security Guard
lines.text: Uh, excuse me folks this is a uhh...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Closed set. We know but we're friends with the monkey. [guard lets them in]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good morning. Hey pal, look who I brought. It's your old friend Harry Elefante. [Marcel grabs the elephant doll and throws it to the ground]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Woah, dude, burn.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't get it, he seemed so happy to see me yesterday.

lines.speaker: Trainer
lines.text: Hey don't take it personal, he's under a lot of pressure, ya know, starring in a movie and all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now just how big of a star is Marcel?

lines.speaker: Trainer
lines.text: In human terms, I'd say Cybill Shepard.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Woah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: [to guys wering yellow isolation suits] So, are you guys in the movie, or are you just really paranoid.

lines.speaker: Director'S Assistant
lines.text: Hey Sal, Jerry wants to know if the monkey's ready for the subway set?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, excuse me. Jerry is the director, which one's he?

lines.speaker: Director'S Assistant
lines.text: The one in the director's chair.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Gotcha. Phoebs, walk with me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK. Um, how come I'm walking with you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, we're, we're just goin' over here so that we can get away from the horrible flesh eating virus, for the love of God woman, listen to me. Is he lookin', is he lookin'?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: We've got a problem.

lines.speaker: Director'S Assistant
lines.text: Tell me.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: I can't do Chris's makeup. She refuses to acknowledge that she has a moustasche.

lines.speaker: Director'S Assistant
lines.text: Is it bad?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: It looks like one of her eyebrows fell down. Now unless someone convinces her to let me bleach it, Jean-Claude Van Damme is gonna be making out with Gabe Kaplan.

lines.speaker: Director'S Assistant
lines.text: I'll talk to her.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: I hate actors.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: [to an extra in fatigues] Nice camoflauge man, for a minute there I almost didn't see ya.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Excuse me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahhhh.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Uh, is your name Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, yes, yes it is.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Chandler Bing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you know me or are you just really good at this game?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: I'm Susie Moss. Fourth grade, glasses, I used to carry around a box of animal crackers like a purse.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Susie Moss, right, yeah, wow, you look. . . great job growing up.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: It's nice to see you're not still wearing that denim cap with all the little mirrors on it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, right, well yeah, I graduated fourth grade and realized I wasn't a pimp.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Remember the class play? You, you pulled up my skirt and the entire auditorium saw my underpants.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, back then I, uh, used humor as a defense mechanism. Thank God I don't do that anymore.
[cut to Monica and Rachel walking through the set]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What what what what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Jean-Claude Van Damme. I didn't know he was in this movie, he is so hot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ya think?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The muscles from Brussels, wham bam Van Damme, did you see Time Cop?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, was he any good in it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, he like, totally changed time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow, so why don't you go talk to him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What, so you go over there, you tell him you think he's cute, what's the worst that could happen?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He could hear me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, I'm doin' it for ya.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh Rachel don't, don't you dare, don't, don't. Tell him I cook.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me. Hi.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, this is gonna sound kinda goofy but uhhm, my friend over there, who cooks by the way, um, she thinks you're cute.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: You don't think I'm cute?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I, I don't know, um, do you think you're cute? OK, we're kinda gettin' off the track here. Um, I was supposed to come here and tell you my friend thinks you're cute. So what should I tell her?

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: You can tell her I think her friend is cute.
[back to Chandler and Susie]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, Stephen Hurs was the kid who would eat anything for money, David Stein was the guy who had no elbows.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: OK, well then who was the kid that got caught masturbating?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK that's not what he was doing. Alright, he was looking for his bus money.
[a voice in the background calls for makeup]

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Oh that's me, I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh uh, o, OK.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Um, so listen, how many times am I gonna have to touch you on the arm before you ask me on a date.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, uh, let's try one more. . . there you go, say Ernie's, 8 o'clock.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: I'll be there, and who knows, if things go well, maybe this time I'll get to see your underwear.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No one was around to hear that?
[back to Rachel and Monica]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So what'd he say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Agh, what a jerk. I kept talking about you and he kept asking me out. I mean, naturally, you know, I said no.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He just kept asking, and asking, and asking, and asking, and asking, and asking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel if you, if you want to go out with him, you can. Sound like a big jerk to me but if that's what you want to do...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Jean-Claude she said yes, I'll see you tonight. Thank you.
Central Perk. Joey and the girls are sitting at the couches.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And then Jean-Claude took me to that place Crossroads and that's where we hung out with Drew Barrymore.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh man, she's so smokin, she has got the greatest set of. . . no guys around, huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Does anybody need anything?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I'll have an espresso. Oh acutally, I'll get it. If I ask you to, you'll probably end up drinking it yourself.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is so unfair.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. Oh, like you would drink her coffee after what you did to her with Van Damme.
[Ross enters]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Joey I have to cancel racketball for tonight, that was Marcel's trainer. He's gonna let me have him for a couple of hours.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're blowin' me off for a monkey?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, we can rescedule for Saturday.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, unless you hook up with a bunch of pigeons.
[Chandler enters]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, stick a fork in me, I am done.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Stick a fork what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Like, when you're cooking a steak.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, OK, I don't eat meat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well then, how do you know when vegetables are done?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well you know, you juist, you eat them and you can tell.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK, then, eat me, I'm done.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I've met the perfect woman. OK, we're sitting on her couch, we're fooling around, and then suddenly she turns to me and says, 'Do you ever want to do it in an elevator?'

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What did you say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, I believe my exact words were, 'Flaign,en - sten'. I mean I didn't know what to say, how do you know if you wanna do it on an elevator?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you just know.
Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Susie are making out on the couch.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Oh, shoot, we gotta go, got a reservation in 30 minutes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no no no no, no no no no no no, you see, what I had planned shouldn't take more that 2, 3 minutes tops.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Oh, 200 seconds of passion. We gotta go.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: But um, here's an idea, have you ever worn women's underwear?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, ye, yes, actually, but, uh, they were my Aunt Edna's, and there were three of us in there.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Well I was thinking it would be um, kind of sexy if you wore mine tonight, at dinner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You want me to wear your panties?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Could ya?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, if I was wearing your underwear then, uh, what would you be wearing?. . . You're swell.
Ross's apartment. Ross is setting up for his time with Marcel. Joey is there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK, got the music, got the dinner. Oh and check it out, I made Marcel's favorite dish, banannacake.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oooh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: With mealworms.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yaaahhh. Ahhh, candles. What'dya thinks gonna happen here tonight?
[phone rings]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello. Oh hi, are you on your way ove-. Oh. No, no, I, I understand, I mean a monkey's gotta work. No it, it's no big deal, it' not like I uh, had anything special planned. Yeah OK, OK. OK, OK, bye.
Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Rachel are upset with each other. Phoebe is mediating.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, Rachel, why don't you start talking first.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, I feel that this is totally unjustified. [Monica starts making faces behind her back] She gave me the green light, I did nothing but-. Do you think I can't see you in the TV set?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright Monica, if there is something that you would like to share...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ya know, you had no right to go out with him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is the most ridiculous.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You sold me out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I did not sell you out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes you did. Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Would you let me talk. [flicks Monica on the forehead]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you just flick me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, well, you wouldn't let me finish and I was jus- [Monica flicks her back] Ow. That hurt [flicks Monica]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Quit flicking [flicks]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow, you stop flicking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You flicked me first.
[They keep flicking each other. This turns into slapping each other. This leads to wrestling on the floor. All the while Phoebe is saying "Happy thoughts". Eventually Phoebe gets fed up.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, now I'm gonna kick some ass.
[Phoebe grabs each of them by an ear]

lines.speaker: Monica And Rachel
lines.text: Ow. Ow. Ow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, now I will let go if you both stop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, what do you, you want me to stop seeing him, is that what you want?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You want me to just call him up and tell him that you're seeing him instead? That's what you want?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh that's what you want.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine,

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There we go.You know what, if we were in prison, you guys would be, like, my bitches.
A fancy restraunt (Marcel's). Joey, Ross, Chandler, Susie, and the Director's Assistant are there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks for letting me tag along tonight you guys.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Forget about it.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: How you doin there squirmy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm hangin in. . . and a little out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, assistant to the director. That's a really exciting job, I mean, you must have a ton of cool responsibilities.

lines.speaker: Director'S Assistant
lines.text: I have nothing to do with casting.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what're you guys gonna eat?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: How come all I can think about is putting that ice in my mouth and licking you all over?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because I went to an all boys high school and God is making up for it.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: I want you right here, right now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right now, right here. Don't ya think we're in kind of a public plaaaa [Susie grabs him under the table] They do have the shrimp.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Meet me in the bathroom. [she leaves for the bathroom]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm going to the bathroom now. [leaves for the bathroom]
[In the bathroom Susie and Chandler are kissing. She backs into a stall.]

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: C'mon.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe we're doing this.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Alright mister, let's see those panties.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alrighty. [we see Chandler's pants drop from under the stall door]

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Ooh. Ooh. But ya know what would be even sexier?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: If you didn't have your shirt tucked into them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Alright. Now I would like to see you wearing nothing but them. Take your clothes off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK, but uh, I hope you realize this means we're gonna miss hearing about the specials.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: C'mon hurry, hurry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, do you want this done quick, or do you want this done right?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Alright, turn around. Time to see you from behind.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK.
[She turns him facing the toilet and sneaks out of the stall and gathers up his clothes.]

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Oh, somebody's been doing his buns of steel video.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you want me to uh, clench anything, or-... Susie? Susie.

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: This is for the fourth grade.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Huh? Where, whaddya mean?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Whaddo I mean. Whaddya mean, whaddo I mean? I mean underpants, mister, that's what I mean.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What, what's what you mean?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: My skirt, you lifted, kids laughing. I was Susie Underpants 'till I was 18.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was in the fourth grade. How could you still be upset about that?

lines.speaker: Susie
lines.text: Well um, why don't you call me in 20 years and tell me if you're still upset about this. [she leaves with his clothes]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright, I hope you realize you're not getting these underpants back.
Monica and Van Damme are walking down the street.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe this, just like 2 weeks ago I was watching Sudden Death, now I'm on a date with Jean-Claude Van Damme. Can you beat up that guy? [he nods] Can you beat up that guy?

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so wild. Ya know, I gotta admit, I was kinda surprised that you agreed to go on a blind date.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Normally, I would not do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what made you make the exception for me?

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: 'Cause Rachel told me uh, you were dying to have a threesome with me and uh, Drew Barrymore. By the way, Drew has some groundrules and...
Back at Monica and Rachel's apartment. They are now fighting about what Rachel told Van Damme.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Say you're sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. [hitting each other]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Say it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. [hitting again]
[Monica grabs Rachel by the sweater. Rachel squirms out of it]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, you say you're sorry or your sweater gets it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, OK, that is my favorite sweater, that is my third date sweater.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Say you're sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, you wanna play? OK, let's play, let's play. [She grabs a jar of tomato sauce and Monica's purse]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What're you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You give me back my sweater or it's handbag marinara.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't have the guts.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. Well, at least I wasn't too chicken to tell some guy I thought he was cute.
[Monica pulls a thread on Rachel's sweater and Rachel dumps the tomato sauce in Monica's purse]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh alright, stop, STOP THE MADNESS. This is crazy. Who can even remember why this even started in the first place?
[Monica and Rachel start yelling at the same time]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes that's right. But still, I-, look at your purse, look at your sweater, look at yourselves.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll help you fix your sweater.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'll help you throw out your purse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry that I made you stop seeing him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I'm sorry I went out with him when I knew you liked him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry that I borrowed your gloves [pulls Rachel's gloves out of her purse]
Back at the restroom at Marcel's. Chandler is still in the stall, Joey comes in to go to the bathroom.
[Joey starts whistling, Chandler finishes the tune. Joey whistles again.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ma?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler? What're you still doin' here, I though you guys took off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, no no no, she took off with my clothes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you naked in there?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not exactly. . . I'm wearin panties.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh, you uh, you always wear panties?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, this is the first time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow, talk about your bad luck, I mean, the first time you try panties and someone walks off with your clothes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was not trying them out, Susie asked me to wear them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, let me see.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. I'm not letting you or anybody else see, ever.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, alright. [climbs up in the next stall and looks over at Chandler] Woah, someone's flossing.
[Ross enters, sees Joey in the mirror]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [to Joey who's looking over a toilet stall] Joey, some people don't like that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler's wearing panties.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Let me see. [climbs up in the other adjoining stall]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, you don't have to see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Tushie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright, one of you give me your underpants.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can't help you, I'm not wearing any.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How can you not be wearing any underwear?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I'm gettin' heat from the guy in the hot pink thong.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright look Ross I'll give you 50 dollars for your underpants.
[Some guy has entered.]

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi.
[Back at the table. Joey and Ross return. Shortly, Chandler comes out, holding the stall door in front of him, and leaves.]
Central Perk. Ross, Phoebe, and Chandler are sitting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Phoebs, can I have the milk after you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm almost done with it, keep your panties on.
[Joey enters]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, and I'm in the movie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: One of the virus victims called in sick, so Cathy recommended me and boom, I'm dying on the gurney. Oh Ross, Marcel just finished his last scene if you want to go down there and say goodbye.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahh, oh that's OK, I mean, he's probably got, you know, parties to go to and stuff. But, ya know, he's moved on. Hey, that, that's the way it goes right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: [sees Marcel at the window] Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?
["Looks Like We Made It" starts playing and we enter a whole sequence of Marcel and Ross having fun in the city.]
The movie set. Monica and Rachel are saying goodbye to Jean-Claude

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: [to Rachel] I'm sorry it didn't work out between you and me, [to Monica] or you and me. Drew was very disappointed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, well, bye. [kisses him]

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, bye for me too. [kisses him]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, well, bye-bye again. [kisses him again]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Perhaps, uh, the three of us, just could. . .

lines.speaker: Monica And Rachel
lines.text: Oh, no no no no no.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Are you sure, I can crush a walnut with my butt.

lines.speaker: Monica And Rachel
lines.text: No no no no.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Impressive.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But no. Maybe if I were baking.

lines.speaker: Monica And Rachel
lines.text: Bye-bye.
City street. Whole gang is there seeing off Marcel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bye Marcel. See ya on the big screen. You keep people drinkin' that beer, OK. I miss you buddy.
[Marcel is driven off in a limo]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know, I think I want to write a song about all this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Oh, except one of the strings on my guitar is broken. Hey, Chandler, can I borrow your G-string?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How long you been waitin' to say that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: About 20 minutes. CLOSING CREDITS
Filming a scene from the movie. Joey is dying on a gurney, Van Damme is looking over him.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Can't you see what's going on here, this man is dying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aaaaagggghhhhh.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Cut.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Can't you see what's going on here, this man is dying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aaaaagggghhhhh.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Cut.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Can't you see what's going on here, this man is dying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aaaaagggghhhhh. Mommy

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Cut.

lines.speaker: Van Damme
lines.text: Can't you see what's going on here, this man is dead.
END
